title: C++ Constants
content:
### Constants in C++

Constants are variables whose values cannot be changed after initialization.

---

### The `const` Keyword

```cpp
const double PI = 3.14159;
const int MAX_USERS = 100;
// PI = 3.14; // ERROR: cannot modify const
```

---

### The `constexpr` Keyword (C++11)

Compile-time evaluation:

```cpp
constexpr int square(int x) {
  return x * x;
}

constexpr int VALUE = square(5); // Evaluated at compile time
```

---

### The `#define` Preprocessor

```cpp
#define PI 3.14159
#define MAX 100

int area = PI * r * r;
```

---

### Comparison

| Method | Keyword/Directive | When Evaluated | Type-safe |
|--------|------------------|----------------|-----------|
| const | `const` | Runtime | Yes |
| constexpr | `constexpr` | Compile-time | Yes |
| define | `#define` | Preprocessing | No |

---

### Enum Constants

```cpp
enum Color { RED, GREEN, BLUE };
enum class Status { OK = 200, NOT_FOUND = 404 }; // C++11 scoped enum
```

---

> **Key Point:** Prefer `const` and `constexpr` over `#define` for constants. They provide type safety and are visible to the debugger.

quiz.question: Which keyword creates a compile-time constant?
